summary: U.S. Central Command conducted strikes against multiple military targets in Iran, including surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone maintenance facilities, and mine-laying capabilities, in response to an Iranian drone attack on a commercial tanker in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's IRGC retaliated with missile and drone strikes against U.S. military infrastructure at Kuwait's Ali Al-Salem base and the Fifth Fleet headquarters at Bahrain's Salman Port. Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in southern Lebanese territory as long as a threat persists, while the IDF continued operations against Hezbollah in Nabatieh and against Hamas commanders in Gaza.
key_points: U.S. CENTCOM struck Iranian military surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone maintenance facilities, and mine-laying capabilities in the Strait of Hormuz area in response to an Iranian drone attack on a commercial tanker. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)

IRGC naval and aerospace units launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting eight U.S. military infrastructures at Ali Al-Salem base in Kuwait and the Fifth Fleet at Salman Port in Bahrain. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)

Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a threat is present; a security annex allows IDF to verify Lebanese Army dismantling of Hezbollah infrastructure. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93273)

IDF Egoz unit, under Division 36, identified and eliminated several Hezbollah terrorists armed with RPGs operating near the security zone in Nabatieh, and a multi-dimensional unit destroyed a Hezbollah launcher in the area. (Source: @New_security8200/202124)

IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Walid al-Naha, deputy commander of a Nukhba squad that led the October 7 infiltration into Israeli territory and participated in directing the kidnapping of Israeli citizens into Gaza. (Source: @New_security8200/202116)
IDF Division 210 forces eliminated two armed terrorists in the security zone in southern Syria, south of the village of Khader, in a close-range engagement. (Source: @inon_yttach/9187)

66 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts declared Trump and Netanyahu 'condemned to death' and called for the nuclear file to be removed from negotiations and for the Strait of Hormuz to remain closed. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)
Kuwait's Ministry of Defense announced its armed forces intercepted two hostile ballistic missiles in Kuwaiti airspace at dawn, with no injuries or damage reported. (Source: @shin_persian/7255)

sections.executive_summary: U.S. CENTCOM conducted retaliatory strikes against multiple Iranian military targets in the Strait of Hormuz area following an Iranian drone attack on a commercial tanker, while Iran's IRGC responded with missile and drone strikes on U.S. bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement allowing the IDF to remain in southern Lebanon as long as threats persist, with a security annex enabling Israeli verification of Hezbollah infrastructure dismantling. The IDF continued targeted killings of senior Hamas and Hezbollah operatives in Gaza and southern Lebanon.

points: U.S. CENTCOM forces struck multiple Iranian military targets in the Strait of Hormuz area, including military surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone maintenance facilities, and mine-laying capabilities, in response to an Iranian drone attack on a commercial tanker. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)

IRGC announced 'decisive missile and drone operations in response to US aggression,' targeting eight U.S. military infrastructures at Ali Al-Salem base in Kuwait and the Fifth Fleet at Salman Port in Bahrain using ballistic missiles and drones. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)

66 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts issued a statement declaring U.S. President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu 'condemned to death,' making their killing obligatory on anyone with access, while calling for the nuclear file to be removed from negotiations and for the Strait of Hormuz to remain closed. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)
Iranian state media (IRIB) reported that passage through the Strait of Hormuz still requires coordination with the Islamic Revolutionary Guards. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4466)
Fars Province Army announced UXO disposal operations around Shiraz Airport for one week starting June 28, warning that explosion sounds may be heard. (Source: @shin_persian/7262)

India's maritime regulatory body canceled its previous warning restricting Indian vessel transit through the Persian Gulf region, including the Strait of Hormuz, and the deployment of Indian seafarers in conflict zones. (Source: @SharghDaily/168592)

Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a threat is present; the security annex allows the IDF to enter and verify that the Lebanese Army has indeed dismantled Hezbollah infrastructure in the area. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93273)
IDF Chief of Staff expressed support for the Lebanon agreement, calling it historic and significant, and stated that the test now is implementation by both sides. (Source: @amitsegal/62595)

Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that army commanders and soldiers will continue to operate resolutely in Lebanon to remove threats and ensure the security of northern residents. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4467)

IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Walid al-Naha, deputy commander of a Hamas Nukhba squad who commanded the October 7 infiltration into Israeli territory and participated in directing the kidnapping of Israeli citizens into Gaza; he was the nephew of former Hamas Political Bureau member Ismail al-Naha. (Source: @New_security8200/202116)

IDF eliminated Abd al-Rahman Mah Abd al-Karim Ziyada, commander of a Nukhba terrorist squad, in the Shati area; he had infiltrated Israeli territory on October 7 and looted IDF vehicles into Gaza. In a separate strike in Khan Younis, IDF eliminated Kamal Muhammad Hamdan Nagar, head of the tunneling unit in Khan Younis. (Source: @idf_telegram/24460)
Sergeant First Class David Hazut, 21, from Ashkelon, a platoon commander in Battalion 12, Golani Brigade, was killed in a clash with terrorists in southern Lebanon. (Source: @yediotnews25/132010)
Israeli Finance Minister Smotrich stated regarding the Israel-Lebanon agreement: 'We obtained legitimacy to remain in southern Lebanon.' (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373358)

IDF eliminated Abd al-Rahman Mah Abd al-Karim Ziyada, commander of a Nukhba terrorist squad in Hamas's military wing, in the Shati area; he infiltrated Israeli territory on October 7 and looted IDF vehicles into the Gaza Strip. (Source: @idf_telegram/24460)

IDF eliminated Kamal Muhammad Hamdan Nagar, head of the tunneling unit in the Khan Younis area within Hamas's military wing, in a separate strike on the same day in Khan Younis. (Source: @idf_telegram/24460)
IDF Southern Command forces are deployed in the area in accordance with the agreement and will continue operating to remove any immediate threat. (Source: @idf_telegram/24460)

Hamas terrorists have been attempting to advance terror plots against IDF forces operating in the Gaza Strip throughout the war, particularly in the recent period, and were eliminated in precise strikes. (Source: @idf_telegram/24460)

Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement under which the IDF will remain in Lebanese territory as long as a threat is present; a security annex stipulates that the IDF will enter to verify the Lebanese Army has dismantled Hezbollah infrastructure in the area, following incidents where the Lebanese Army provided false reports about infrastructure clearance. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93273)

IDF Egoz unit, under Division 36, identified and eliminated several Hezbollah terrorists armed with RPGs operating in the Nabatieh area near the security zone where IDF forces operate in southern Lebanon; the Air Force struck and eliminated the terrorists and their structure. (Source: @New_security8200/202124)
A multi-dimensional unit destroyed a Hezbollah launcher in the Nabatieh area that posed a threat to IDF forces. (Source: @New_security8200/202124)
Sergeant First Class David Hazut of the Golani Brigade was killed in a clash with terrorists in the Dir Siryan area of southern Lebanon. (Source: @amitsegal/62590)

A Lebanese military source stated that the Lebanese Army is a national institution subject exclusively to Lebanese law, rejected Israeli claims about disloyal elements within the army, and affirmed that all personnel perform their duty with loyalty to the military institution and the nation. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373355)

IDF Division 210 forces identified and eliminated two armed terrorists in the security zone in southern Syria, south of the village of Khader, in a close-range engagement; both were known to the security establishment. (Source: @inon_yttach/9187)

IDF Etzioni Brigade (6), under Division 210, eliminated several armed terrorists in the security zone in southern Syria; the IDF will continue operating in the security zone in southern Syria and removing any threat to Israeli citizens and IDF forces. (Source: @idf_telegram/24459)

Anti-corruption arrest operations are ongoing in Iraq, with security elements raiding the homes of arrested officials in Baghdad and other Iraqi cities; those detained include parliament members and a deputy oil ministry official. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80231)
Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi and his Iraqi counterpart Fuad Hussein held a meeting in Baghdad. (Source: @presstv/195990)
IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting eight U.S. military infrastructures at Ali Al-Salem base in Kuwait and the Fifth Fleet at Salman Port in Bahrain. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)
Kuwait's Ministry of Defense announced its armed forces intercepted two hostile ballistic missiles in Kuwaiti airspace at dawn, with no injuries or damage reported. (Source: @shin_persian/7255)
Arab reports indicated explosions in Bahrain following Iranian fire. (Source: @New_security8200/202122)

India's maritime regulatory body canceled its previous restrictions on Indian vessel transit through the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz and on deploying Indian seafarers in conflict zones. (Source: @SharghDaily/168592)

key_developments: U.S. CENTCOM struck Iranian military surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone maintenance facilities, and mine-laying capabilities in the Strait of Hormuz area in response to an Iranian drone attack on a commercial tanker. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)
IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones at eight U.S. military infrastructures at Ali Al-Salem base in Kuwait and the Fifth Fleet at Salman Port in Bahrain. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)

Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement allowing IDF to remain in southern Lebanon as long as threats persist, with a security annex enabling Israeli verification of Hezbollah infrastructure dismantlement by the Lebanese Army. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93273)
IDF Egoz unit under Division 36 eliminated Hezbollah terrorists armed with RPGs in Nabatieh near the security zone and destroyed a Hezbollah launcher in the area. (Source: @New_security8200/202124)

IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Walid al-Naha, deputy commander of a Hamas Nukhba squad who commanded the October 7 infiltration and participated in kidnapping Israeli citizens into Gaza. (Source: @New_security8200/202116)
IDF Division 210 eliminated two armed terrorists in the security zone in southern Syria, south of the village of Khader, in a close-range engagement. (Source: @inon_yttach/9187)

66 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts declared Trump and Netanyahu condemned to death and called for the nuclear file to be removed from negotiations and the Strait of Hormuz to remain closed. (Source: @mamlekate/103770)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict has escalated significantly with direct U.S.-Iran military exchanges spanning the Strait of Hormuz to the Gulf states, raising the risk of broader regional war. Iran's IRGC demonstrated willingness to strike U.S. bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, while Kuwait intercepted two ballistic missiles, indicating the conflict is expanding beyond the immediate Israel-Iran-Lebanon theater. The Israel-Lebanon framework agreement introduces a new operational framework for IDF presence in southern Lebanon, but Hezbollah continues armed operations in Nabatieh, suggesting the agreement faces immediate implementation challenges. Senior Hamas commanders remain priority targets for the IDF in Gaza, indicating continued high-intensity counterterrorism operations.
sections.regional_response: Kuwait's military successfully intercepted incoming ballistic missiles, demonstrating active defense of its territory against Iranian strikes. India reversed its maritime restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a calculated assessment that transit risks may be manageable. Iran's Assembly of Experts escalated rhetoric by declaring Trump and Netanyahu condemned to death, while simultaneously demanding the nuclear file be removed from negotiations, hardening Iran's diplomatic posture. Iraq's government held an emergency cabinet session amid an anti-corruption crackdown targeting senior officials, suggesting internal political instability concurrent with regional tensions.
sections.intelligence_notes: The U.S. strikes on Iranian mine-laying capabilities and drone maintenance facilities suggest an effort to degrade Iran's asymmetric naval warfare capacity in the Strait of Hormuz. The Israel-Lebanon security annex specifically addresses past Lebanese Army deception regarding Hezbollah infrastructure clearance, indicating Israeli intelligence has documented systematic false reporting. Iran's dual-track approach—military retaliation against U.S. bases combined with the Assembly of Experts' assassination fatwa—signals coordination between IRGC military operations and clerical establishment messaging. The ongoing anti-corruption arrests in Iraq targeting parliament members and oil ministry officials may reflect internal power struggles that could affect Iraq's positioning in the regional conflict.
